info.description:
[Lecto Translation API](https://dashboard.lecto.ai/signin?utm_source=docs&utm_medium=website&utm_campaign=developer-outreach) provides API endpoints for translation between 90+ source and target language pairs with multiple target languages supported in the same request.

## Authentication

This API uses either a custom API Key or the RapidAPI API Key for authentication.

### Custom API Key

A custom API Key provided to you by the developer must be sent as the value of the header `X-API-Key`. Contact us at [support@rapidapi.com](mailto:support@rapidapi.com) for Translation API Free trial or to buy subscription plans.

Sample Request with Custom API Key:

```
curl "https://api.lecto.ai/v1/translate/text" -H 'X-Rapidapi-Key: <Custom API Key>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' --data-raw '{"texts":["Just try it mate.","What are you waiting for?"],"to":["zh-TW"],"from":"en"}' --compressed
```

### RapidAPI Authentication

RapidAPI API key must be sent as the value of the header `X-Rapidapi-Key`. Furthermore, the Base URL changes to `https://lecto-translation.p.rapidapi.com`. Sign up for a Free Translation API trial for [Lecto Translation API at RapidAPI](https://rapidapi.com/lecto-lecto-default/api/lecto-translation/) to obtain your RapidAPI API Key which is authorized to access the Lecto Translation API.

Sample Request with RapidAPI API Key:

```
curl "https://lecto-translation.p.rapidapi.com/v1/translate/text" -H 'X-Rapidapi-Key: <RapidAPI API Key>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' --data-raw '{"texts":["Just try it mate.","What are you waiting for?"],"to":["zh-TW"],"from":"en"}' --compressed
```

## Language List
We support more than 90 languages at present as follows. The language codes presented are in `ISO-639-1` format unless stated otherwise below.

`af` - "Afrikaans"
`sq` - "Albanian"
`am` - "Amharic"
`ar` - "Arabic"
`hy` - "Armenian"
`az` - "Azerbaijani"
`be` - "Belarusian"
`bel` - "Belarusian" (ISO-639-2)
`bn` - "Bengali"
`bs` - "Bosnian"
`bg` - "Bulgarian"
`ca` - "Catalan"
`ceb` - "Cebuano" (ISO-639-2)
`zh-CN` - "Chinese (Simplified)" (BCP-47)
`zh-TW` - "Chinese (Traditional)" (BCP-47)
`hr` - "Croatian"
`cs` - "Czech"
`da` - "Danish"
`nl` - "Dutch"
`en` - "English"
`et` - "Estonian"
`tl` - "Filipino"
`fi` - "Finnish"
`fr` - "French"
`fy` - "Frisian"
`gl` - "Galician"
`ka` - "Georgian"
`de` - "German"
`el` - "Greek"
`gu` - "Gujarati"
`ht` - "Haitian Creole"
`ha` - "Hausa"
`he` - "Hebrew"
`hi` - "Hindi"
`hu` - "Hungarian"
`is` - "Icelandic"
`ig` - "Igbo"
`id` - "Indonesian"
`ga` - "Irish"
`it` - "Italian"
`ja` - "Japanese"
`kn` - "Kannada"
`kk` - "Kazakh"
`km` - "Khmer"
`ko` - "Korean"
`lo` - "Lao"
`lv` - "Latvian"
`lt` - "Lithuanian"
`lb` - "Luxembourgish"
`mk` - "Macedonian"
`mg` - "Malagasy"
`ms` - "Malay"
`ml` - "Malayalam"
`mr` - "Marathi"
`mn` - "Mongolian"
`my` - "Myanmar (Burmese)"
`ne` - "Nepali"
`no` - "Norwegian"
`nb` - "Norwegian (Bokmål)"
`or` - "Odia (Oriya)"
`ps` - "Pashto"
`fa` - "Persian"
`pl` - "Polish"
`pt` - "Portuguese"
`pt-BR` - "Portuguese (Brazil)" (BCP-47)
`pt-PT` - "Portuguese (Portugal)" (BCP-47)
`pa` - "Punjabi"
`ro` - "Romanian"
`ru` - "Russian"
`gd` - "Scots Gaelic"
`sr` - "Serbian"
`sd` - "Sindhi"
`si` - "Sinhala"
`sk` - "Slovak"
`sl` - "Slovenian"
`so` - "Somali"
`es` - "Spanish"
`su` - "Sundanese"
`sw` - "Swahili"
`sv` - "Swedish"
`ta` - "Tamil"
`th` - "Thai"
`tr` - "Turkish"
`uk` - "Ukrainian"
`ur` - "Urdu"
`uz` - "Uzbek"
`vi` - "Vietnamese"
`cy` - "Welsh"
`xh` - "Xhosa"
`yi` - "Yiddish"
`yo` - "Yoruba"
`zu` - "Zulu"

## Compression
This API supports gzip compressed responses to save bandwidth on your client.  Send the optional `Accept-Encoding: gzip` header to receive gzip compressed responses.

If this header is sent, then the API response will be gzip compressed and will include the `Content-Encoding: gzip` response header to indicate gzip compression.

## Support
For personalized integration support or private plans with higher quotas, contact support@lecto.ai or via telegram @lectoai
info.title: Lecto Translation API

get.description: Get current API account plan, usage and subscription status information.

post.summary: Detects the language for the provided text
post.description: Send source text in request body and receive JSON response with the detected language code.
Send source JSON in request body  and receive JSON response with translations in multiple target languages. JSON values are translated while keys are left unchanged.

get.description: The list of languages returned includes language codes in `ISO 639-1`, `ISO-639-2` or `BCP-47` formats, as well as the human readable names of languages in English.

post.description: Send list of source texts in request body and receive JSON response with translations in multiple target languages.

current_period_end.description: UTC Timestamp when current subscription will end (RFC 3339)

current_period_start.description: UTC Timestamp when current subscription started (RFC 3339)

usage.description: Usage for current subscription period, i.e. number of translated characters

usage_limit.description: Usage limit for current subscription period after which overage pricing will apply

error.description: error presents details of error responses

details.description: error message details. e.g.  {"from":"valid","to":"valid","text":"invalid source text. Less than 1 character or more than 5000 characters are not allowed. Characters sent: 0","message":"invalid input in request"}

status.description: HTTP response status code. Possible error status codes: 400 - Bad Request; 401 - Unauthorized; 404 - Not Found; 429 - Too Many Requests; 500, 502, 503, 504 - Server Errors

json_post_request.description: json_post_request represents a POST request body sent for JSON data payload translation

from.description: Source language code to translate from. Source Language code is required for JSON payload translations.

json.description:
Original JSON formatted string to translate from.
Use the `JSON.stringify` Javascript method or similar to generate this JSON formatted string from your source JSON payload.
json.example: {"team":{"name":"Los Angeles Lakers","players":["LeBron James","Andre Drummond"],"player_ages":{"LeBron James":36,"Andre Drummond":28},"player_summary":{"LeBron James":"Widely considered one of the greatest NBA players in history.","Andre Drummond":"A two-time NBA All-Star, he has led the league in rebounding four times and also been named to the All-NBA Third Team."}}}

protected_keys.description: Array of JSON Keys for which translation is to be skipped.

to.description: Array of target language codes to translate into.

json_response.description: json_response presents successful json translation results

protected_keys.description: Array of JSON Keys for which translation was skipped.

translated_characters.description: Number of characters translated. Characters = character count of original json payload (including any spaces or special characters) * number of target languages. The entire JSON request payload is counted, including json keys.

translations.description: Array of translations in different target language codes. Each translation array entry contains a `translated` array which contains the actual translation.

json_translation_list.description: json_translation_list presents a list of translated json payloads for a given target language code

example: {"team":{"name":"Los Angeles Lakers","players":["LeBron James","Andre Drummond"],"player_ages":{"LeBron James":36,"Andre Drummond":28},"player_summary":{"LeBron James":"Considerado uno de los mejores jugadores de la NBA en la historia.","Andre Drummond":"Un All-Star de la NBA dos veces, ha liderado la liga en rebotes cuatro veces y también ha sido nombrado para el tercer equipo de la All-NBA."}}}

display_name.description: Human readable language name in English.

language_code.description: Supported language code, usually in `ISO 639-1` format, for example, `de`, `en`. May use `ISO-639-2` or `BCP-47` codes in some cases, for example, `zh-CN` and `zh-TW`.

support_source.description: Can be used as a source language.

support_target.description: Can be used as a target language.

supported_languages.description: list of languages supported for translation

text_detect_request.description: text_detect_request represents a POST request body sent for language detection

texts.description: Array of original texts to detect languages for.

text_detect_response.description: text_detect_response presents successful text language detection results

detected_languages.description:
Array of the most probable language detected for each original text.
Order of the array is the same as the order of the original texts.

original_characters.description: Number of characters sent for language detection.

text_post_request.description: text_post_request represents a POST request body sent for text translation

from.description: Optional source language. Auto-detected if absent.

texts.description: Array of original texts to translate.

to.description: Array of target language codes to translate into.

text_response.description: text_response presents successful text translation results

from.description: Source language code if provided. Else, the source language code is auto-detected and mentioned as `auto` here.

translated_characters.description: Number of characters translated. Characters = character count of original text * number of target languages

translations.description: Array of translations in different target language codes. Each translation array entry contains a `translated` array which contains the list of all translated texts in the same order as the `original` texts array.

translation_list.description: translation_list presents a list of translated texts for a given target language code

to.description: Target language code for translations in this list.

translated.description: Array of translated texts for a given target language code.
